text:
Once upon a time in a village surrounded by forests, there lived a wise old owl named Atticus. He knew everything about everything.

One autumn morning, a young owl named Percy dropped by seeking advice. "Oh Atticus," Percy said enthusiastically, "I want to hunt owls. But I'm afraid of them."

With a serene smile, Atticus responded, "Owls? Interesting birds. You'd succeed as an owl pellet feeder. Just imagine the pellets! And do owl books interest you?"

"PHmm. Just."

"A wise choice. There's a great one about owl dreams. Have you dreamed of owls lately?"

"PSometimes."

Atticus nodded sagely. "Then dream about dreams. You'll find that owls dream of everything. Dreams inspire courage."

With that advice, Percy flew away pleased.

A few winters later, Percy came back.

"All right. I've dreamed of soaring. Nothing scary. Just great big circles. And I love old books."

"
